text: Fort Tilden is filled with things to do, from surfing and swimming (even an unofficial nude beach), to fishing, bike riding, and listening to your girlfriend complain that "we're lost, and we're gonna die out here, this isn't even funny anymore" as you trek through some of the finest wilderness in Queens. There may not be an abundance of wildlife on the trails, but there are Piping Plovers to swoop at you, seagulls to steal your lunch, and hepitutus needles in the sand, so you'll always be on your toes. One of the best spots in NYC if you're looking to roll fitness, leisure and an accidental diseased injection all into one. 7/10 - will need to come back and look for more cans of rolling rock from 1996 in the weeds.
name: Fort Tilden (Gateway National Recreation Area)

text: Statue of Liberty, formally Liberty Enlightening the World, colossal statue on Liberty Island in the Upper New York Bay, U.S., commemorating the friendship of the peoples of the United States and France. Standing 305 feet (93 metres) high including its pedestal, it represents a woman holding a torch in her raised right hand and a tablet bearing the adoption date of the Declaration of Independence (July 4, 1776) in her left. The torch, which measures 29 feet (8.8 metres) from the flame tip to the bottom of the handle, is accessible via a 42-foot (12.8-metre) service ladder inside the arm (this ascent was open to the public from 1886 to 1916). An elevator carries visitors to the observation deck in the pedestal, which may also be reached by stairway, and a spiral staircase leads to an observation platform in the figure’s crown. A plaque at the pedestal’s entrance is inscribed with a sonnet, “The New Colossus” (1883) by Emma Lazarus.
name: Statue of Liberty National Monument

text: One World Trade Center One World Trade Center logo.svg One World Trade Center Fähre.jpg Wikimedia | OpenStreetMap Alternative names 1 WTC Freedom Tower (pre-2009)[1] Record height Tallest in North America since 2013[I] Preceded by Willis Tower General information Status Complete Type OfficeObservationCommunication Architectural style Contemporary modern Location 285 Fulton Street Manhattan, New York City Coordinates 40°42′46.8″N 74°0′48.6″WCoordinates: 40°42′46.8″N 74°0′48.6″W Construction started April 27, 2006 Topped-out May 10, 2013[2] Opened November 3, 2014[3][4] May 29, 2015 (One World Observatory)[5] Cost US$3.9 billiona [6][7] Height Architectural 1,776 ft (541.3 m)[8][9] Tip 1,792 ft (546.2 m)[8] Roof 1,368 ft (417.0 m)[10] Top floor 1,268 ft (386.5 m)[8] Observatory 1,254 ft (382.2 m)[8] Technical details Floor count 94 (+5 below ground floors)[8][11] Floor area 3,501,274 sq ft (325,279 m2)[8] Lifts/elevators 73,[8] made by ThyssenKrupp.[12] Design and construction Architect David Childsb Daniel Libeskind (2002)[13] Developer Port Authority of New York and New Jersey[8] Structural engineer WSP Cantor Seinuk Other designers Hill International, The Louis Berger Group[14] Main contractor Tishman Construction References [8][15] a. April 2012 estimate. b. Skidmore, Owings & Merrill. One World Trade Center (also known as One WTC, 1 World Trade Center, 1 WTC, or Freedom Tower[16]) is the main building of the rebuilt World Trade Center complex in Lower Manhattan, New York City. One WTC is the tallest building in the United States, the tallest building in the Western Hemisphere, and the sixth-tallest in the world. The supertall structure has the same name as the North Tower of the original World Trade Center, which was destroyed in the terrorist attacks of September 11, 2001. The new skyscraper stands on the northwest corner of the 16-acre (6.5 ha) World Trade Center site, on the site of the original 6 World Trade Center. The building is bounded by West Street to the west, Vesey Street to the north, Fulton Street to the south, and Washington Street to the east. The building's architect is David Childs, whose firm Skidmore, Owings & Merrill (SOM) also designed the Burj Khalifa and the Willis Tower. The construction of below-ground utility relocations, footings, and foundations for the new building began on April 27, 2006. One World Trade Center became the tallest structure in New York City on April 30, 2012, when it surpassed the height of the Empire State Building. The tower's steel structure was topped out on August 30, 2012. On May 10, 2013, the final component of the skyscraper's spire was installed, making the building, including its spire, reach a total height of 1,776 feet (541 m). Its height in feet is a deliberate reference to the year when the United States Declaration of Independence was signed. The building opened on November 3, 2014;[4] the One World Observatory opened on May 29, 2015.[5]
name: One World Trade Center

text: I remember going to this building back in the day when it housed the Whitney Museum of Modern Art. I must say, Met Breuer made a great choice of space to display the modern, innovative, and quirky artwork. The fact that it's very well compartmentalized and relatively small, compared to the Metropolitan Museum, really allows you to enjoy and appreciate all the permanent and special exhibits in a more leisurely, much less touristy environment. If you are a local, or a frequent visitor to NYC, make sure to visit the museum every so often, as the special exhibits are changed often and are very well curated. Personally, I am not a fan and am rather sceptical of abstract art, for example, but some pieces of abstract art Met Breuer showcases don't leave me feeling impartial.
name: The Met Breuer

text: The Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) is an art museum located in Midtown Manhattan, New York City, on 53rd Street between Fifth and Sixth Avenues. It plays a major role in developing and collecting modern art, and is often identified as one of the largest and most influential museums of modern art in the world. MoMA's collection offers an overview of modern and contemporary art, including works of architecture and design, drawing, painting, sculpture, photography, prints, illustrated books and artist's books, film, and electronic media. The MoMA Library includes approximately 300,000 books and exhibition catalogs, over 1,000 periodical titles, and over 40,000 files of ephemera about individual artists and groups. The archives hold primary source material related to the history of modern and contemporary art.
name: The Museum of Modern Art

text: Thank you for this beautiful experience. When I thought about attending a gospel ceremony, I imagined these films in which everyone is impeccable ... in the powerful voices raised in prayers ... and that I wanted to live. In NYC there are tours that offer you this option ... DO NOT TAKE IT ... it is not necessary ... Search on the internet and hit this place and thinking about a ceremony at 8 am, encourage me, since the people who attend the community at 8 am must be people who attend for their spiritual convictions and not to fulfill ... I thought so . The arrival is very easy, the green line 6 and you get off at 116 in harlem. You walk 5 streets and voila. I attended the service at 8 am, I dressed appropriately since it did not seem right to attend a religious ceremony in shorts, flip flops and with the tourist look in summer ... plus one for the influence and cinema images of these ceremonies where those who attend they are impeccable and for them it is the most important day of their week ... It is the best experience I have had in New York, the warmth of the people who attend the service, the kindness and attention to the visits, the shepherds with their joyful way of sharing, the music, it is amazing to hear those beautiful voices and feel part of the celebration that takes place. I really did not expect so much emotion and I was very moved by the way they receive the visits, it is a unique experience.a religious in shorts, flip flops and with the tourist look in summer ... but one for the influence and film images of These ceremonies where those who attend go impeccably and for them it is the most important day of their week ... You get off at the 116 station, and five streets away is the place, they welcome you with an incredible smile, they welcome you and then they pass you to the venue ... the ceremony starts at 8 am, so I consider it incorrect to arrive later ... even if there is no problem, tourists arrived equally after 8 am and everyone received them with that impeccable kindness. The service lasts two hours, same that pass in seconds ... you do not feel the passage of time ... it is like an exhalation ... the music .. the joy .. the smiles ... in short, it is an experience that must be lived and felt. My recommendation is to attend this service at 8 am, go well dressed for respect ... enjoy and sing and integrate ... since it is a party ... not a rigid ritual and full of protocols like the ones I've been used to seeing in churches Catholics, everything here is joy and jokes and laughter ... without neglecting a deep sense of spirituality ... Go with the idea of ​​having one of the best moments of your life ... and be willing to feel that good vibes of the place.
name: Bethel Gospel Assembly
